idea.hook: Winds faster than a jet plane, on a planet?!
facts: Neptune holds the record for the fastest winds in our Solar System.
They rage at speeds up to 2,100 km/h (1,300 mph) – faster than the speed of sound on Earth!
These powerful winds are driven by Neptune's internal heat, not the Sun, and blow in complex patterns, sometimes even against the planet's rotation.
idea.payoff: A distant, icy world with its own internal energy source fueling supersonic storms, showcasing a weather system unlike anything on Earth.
idea.title: Neptune's Insane Supersonic Winds! 💨🪐

scenes.text: Winds faster than a jet plane... on a planet?!

scenes.text: Neptune: Fastest Winds in the Solar System.

scenes.text: Up to 2,100 km/h (1,300 mph)! Faster than sound on Earth.

scenes.text: Fueled by Neptune's internal heat, not the Sun. Winds defy rotation!
scenes.visual: Neptune's globe with a subtle glow radiating from its core, suggesting internal heat, with overlayed wind arrows moving in complex, sometimes opposing, directions.

scenes.text: Supersonic storms fueled by internal energy. A truly alien weather system!
